author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
Why the Gift of Faith is Not Given to All.
title: Why the Gift of Faith is Not Given to All.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Faith, then, as well in its beginning as in its completion, is God’s gift; and let no one have any doubt whatever, unless he desires to resist the plainest sacred writings, that this gift is given to some, while to some it is not given.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: But why it is not given to all ought not to disturb the believer, who believes that from one all have gone into a condemnation, which undoubtedly is most righteous; so that even if none were delivered therefrom, there would be no just cause for finding fault with God.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Whence it is plain that it is a great grace for many to be delivered, and to acknowledge in those that are not delivered what would be due to themselves; so that he that glorieth may glory not in his own merits, which he sees to be equalled in those that are condemned, but in the Lord.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: But why He delivers one rather than another,—“His judgments are unsearchable, and His ways past finding out.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: For it is better in this case for us to hear or to say, “O man, who art thou that repliest against God?” than to dare to speak as if we could know what He has chosen to be kept secret.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Since, moreover, He could not will anything unrighteous.
